actor_claims.quote_text: the board and Saks’ senior management periodically review, consider and assess Saks’ operations and financial performance and industry conditions as they may affect Saks’ long-term strategic goals and plans
Goldman Sachs, one of Saks’ longstanding financial advisors
Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz, referred to as Wachtell Lipton, Saks’ external counsel
Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC (a long-time advisor to Saks, referred to as “Morgan Stanley”)

actor_relation_claims.quote_text: Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
Sponsor E would again be joined in its proposal by Sponsor A as a primary participant.
Saks, Hudson’s Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents later on July 28, 2013.

bid_claims.quote_text: On July 24, 2013, representatives of Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson’s Bay was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock

bid_claims.bid_value_unit: per share of common stock in cash
bid_claims.quote_text: the $16 per share of common stock in cash to be paid to Saks’ shareholders in the proposed merger

event_claims.description: Sponsor A made an unsolicited initial contact expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.
event_claims.quote_text: In February 2013, Stephen I. Sadove, Saks’ Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, received an unsolicited phone call from a representative of a private equity firm, which we refer to as Sponsor A, expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.

event_claims.description: Mr. Baker requested a meeting with Mr. Sadove to discuss a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.
event_claims.quote_text: On April 1, 2013, Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker, the Director, Governor, and Chief Executive Officer of Hudson’s Bay, at the request of Mr. Baker and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay.

event_claims.description: Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that it was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock, subject to negotiating a definitive binding agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 24, 2013, representatives of Hudson’s Bay advised Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson’s Bay was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock

event_claims.description: The board authorized management and advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine whether an agreement could be reached.
event_claims.quote_text: The board authorized senior management and Saks’ advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson’s Bay to determine if an agreement could be reached.

participation_count_claims.quote_text: representatives of Goldman Sachs spoke with representatives of each of Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E and were informed that Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).
On July 11, 2013, each of Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor E, together with Sponsor G, on the other hand, submitted proposals expressing their continued interest in an acquisition of Saks.
